properties.remark: HAIL RANGED FROM PEA SIZED TO QUARTER SIZED.
REPORT WAS RELAYED BY MEDIA. PING PONG SIZE HAIL IN DITCH NEAR GARNETT ROAD AND US HIGHWAY 283. TIME BASED ON RADAR.
HAIL ESTIMATED PEA TO QUARTER SIZED.
THE METAL ROOF OF THE COLDWATER LAUNDRY BUILDING WAS TORN OFF.

TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN ACROSS THE ROAD. A PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS BLOWN OVER AND A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE BASE. THIS COULD HAVE BEEN A TORNADO OR STRAIGHT LINE WINDS PENDING A DAMAGE SURVEY.

TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN ACROSS THE ROAD. A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE BASE. AN ENTIRE IRRIGATION PIVOT SYSTEM WAS BLOWN OVER. THIS COULD HAVE BEEN EITHER STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OR A TORNADO PENDING A DAMAGE SURVEY.

TREE LIMBS WERE THROWN ACROSS THE ROAD. A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED OFF NEAR THE BASE. AN ENTIRE IRRIGATION SYSTEM WAS BLOWN OVER. THIS COULD HAVE BEEN STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OR A TORNADO PENDING A DAMAGE SURVEY.
WATER WAS 1 TO 2 FEET OVER HWY 183 ON THE SOUTH-CENTRAL SIDE OF LACROSSE, KS.
THIS IS POST EVENT VERIFICATION FROM SEVERE WEATHER THAT OCCURRED YESTERDAY.
THE OBSERVER NOTED A COUPLE OF BRIEF TOUCHDOWNS OVER A FEW MINUTES.
A 60X120 FOOT METAL BARN WAS DESTROYED, AND CATTLE SHEDS WERE BLOWN DOWN. POWER LINES ALSO WERE BLOWN DOWN.
DAMAGE TO AN OLD SCHOOL HOUSE WAS REPORTED AT THIS LOCATION. DAMAGE TO OUTBUILDINGS WERE ALSO REPORTED ALONG 232 RD.
PUBLIC REPORTED A TORNADO CROSSING HIGHWAY 283 ON THE HODGEMAN AND FORD COUNTY LINE.
THE TORNADO WAS REPORTED AS A CONE TORNADO, FROM A SPOTTER 10 MILES MILES WEST OF ASHLAND.
PUBLIC REPORTED A TORNADO CROSSING HIGHWAY 283 ON THE HODGEMAN AND FORD COUNTY LINE.
THE TORNADO WAS OBSERVED FROM A RESIDENCE 3 MILES SOUTHEAST.

A WALL CLOUD WITH A FUNNEL APPEARING TO REACH TO NEAR THE GROUND WAS OBSERVED FROM A DISTANCE OF ABOUT 6 MILES FROM THE OBSERVATION POINT NORTHEAST OF THE POSSIBLE TORNADO. THE FEATURE APPEARED TO DISSIPATE AND REDEVELOP OVER A 10 MINUTE PERIOD.
SHERIFF REPORTED TORNADO 2 TO 3 MILES WEST OF BURDETTE.

A TORNADO WAS OBSERVED. THE TORNADO DESTROYED NUMEROUS FARM IMPLEMENTS INCLUDING FLIPPING 4 GRAIN AUGERS THROUGH A GRAIN BIN. TWO COMPLETE PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WERE DESTROYED. CONCRETE CATTLE GRATES WERE PULLED FROM THE GROUND AND THROWN 100 FEET ON TOP OF GRAIN BINS.
TORNADO TORE UP STORAGE BINS ON HIS NEIGHBORS FARM.
BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN JUST SOUTH OF THE TOWN OF RUSH CENTER.
THE HAIL SMASHED THOUGH THE SKYLIGHT OF THE SPOTTERS HOME.
GOLFBALL-SIZED HAIL COVERED THE GROUND.
WINDOWS WERE KNOCKED OUT BY HAIL IN KINGSDOWN.
HAIL WAS GOLF BALL TO TENNIS BALL SIZED.

DAMAGE ALONG A TORNADO PATH WAS REPORTED. THE DAMAGE BEGAN AT ABOUT 180TH AVE AND PERSISTED NORTHEASTWARD TO NEAR P AND M ROADS LEADING TO THE STAFFORD COUNTY LINE. DAMAGE TO HOUSES, UTILITY POLES, TREES AND IRRIGATION SYSTEMS WAS OBSERVED.
SHERIFFS DEPUTY REPORTED A LARGE TORNADO JUST NORTH OF ST. JOHN.
STORM CHASER REPORTED A LARGE WEDGE TORNADO TO THE EAST OF HIS POSITION.
MAINLY QUARTER BUT A FEW HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL
THE WIND WAS PICKING UP CONSIDERABLE DUST.
SMALL BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN
THIS WAS A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OF THE TORNADO.
REPORT RECEIVED VIA 800W RADIO.
THIS WAS ONLY A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN.
THE TORNADO WAS RAIN WRAPPED AND ON THE GROUND. IT SOON LIFTED, ONLY TO PRODUCE ANOTHER FUNNEL A SHORT DISTANCE AWAY.
THIS TORNADO ONLY TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY.
TORNADO WAS REPORTED NEAR COLDWATER LAKE.
LOTS OF TREE AND POWER POLE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED. FARM EQUIPMENT WAS ROLLED INTO A BALL. THERE WAS MINOR ROOF AND WINDOW DAMAGE TO THE FARM HOME.
NUMEROUS QUARTER SIZED HAIL STONES WERE OBSERVED.
DIME TO NICKLE SIZED HAIL WITH SOME QUARTER SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED.
DIME TO NICKLE SIZED HAIL WITH SOME QUARTER SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED.
SEVERAL LARGE LIMBS 3 TO 4 INCH IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN OUT OF A TREE LINE. THE CALLER REPORTED THE DAMAGE FROM A GUSTINADO.
THIS TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR DATA.
STOVE PIPE TORNADO
SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED, INCLUDING 6-8 INCH LIMBS BROKE AND UNKNOWN TREES FOUND AT THE PROPERTY. AN OVERHEAD DOOR AND WOODEN AWNING WERE BLOWN AWAY.
ELEPHANT TRUNK TORNADO
WEDGE TORNADO
WEDGE TORNADO
LARGE RAIN WRAPPED WEDGE TORNADO
LARGE TORNADO CONTINUES
SECOND TORNADO HAS DEVELOPED
DAMAGE TO THE GROCERY STORE
CYCLIC REPEATED TORNADOGENESIS
A LARGE TREE WAS ALSO BLOWN DOWN AND POWER POLE SNAPPED WITHIN NESS CITY.
NICKEL SIZE HAIL ALSO REPORTED WITH THIS STORM.
WIDESPREAD TREE AND POWERLINE DAMAGE WAS REPORTED IN HANSTON ALONG WITH A FEW DAMAGED OUT BUILDINGS IN AND AROUND TOWN. THERE WERE ALSO NUMEROUS DOWNED POWER POLES ESPECIALLY JUST SOUTH OF HANSTON.
POWER LINES AND TREES WERE DOWNED AND ROOFS WERE DAMAGED.
WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 60 TO 70 MPH.
THERE WERE FIVE POWER POLES DOWN AND WINDS ESTIMATED AT 70 MPH.
THREE INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMBS BLOWN DOWN. WIND DRIVEN HAIL BROKE OUT A WINDOW ON A PICKUP TOPPER. HAIL SIZE UNKNOWN.
THE REPORT WAS FROM NEAR NESS CITY.
SIX TO SEVEN POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS REPORTED BLOWN DOWN AROUND TOWN.
TWO SEMI TRAILER TRUCKS WERE ROLLED.
AN EMPTY TRUCK TRAILOR WAS BLOWN OVER JUST WEST OF GARDEN CITY AND 1 FOOT DIAMETER TREE LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ON 5TH STREET.
2 FUNNEL CLOUDS WERE REPORTED AS LOWERING TOWARD THE GROUND, BUT TOUCHDOWN COULD NOT BE OBSERVED.
VISIBILITY WAS REDUCED TO NEAR ZERO IN BLOWING DUST.
THE CALLER REPORTED A MIX OF HAIL SIZES FROM PENNY TO NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZE, ALL COMING DOWN MIXED WITH RAIN.
HAIL SIZE VARRIED FROM QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZE.
SPECI KGCK 260201Z AUTO 02043G52KT 1SM TSRA SQ...PK WND 02052/0201...
WINDS ESTIMATE 55-60 MPH
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN BY HIGH WINDS ON 30 ROAD ONE HALF MILE SOUTH OF THE FORD COUNTY LINE.
TREES WERE UPROOTED AND A ROOF WAS POSSIBLY TORN OFF A BUILDING NEAR 118 WRANGLER ROAD. TIME UNCERTAIN AND ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NUMEROUS LIMBS 8-9 INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN DOWN. A POWER LINE WAS BLOWN LOOSE FROM A BUILDING, AND A PICKUP WAS DAMAGED BY LIMBS AND DEBRIS BLOWN AROUND BY THE WIND.
THE WIND GUST WAS RECORDED BY THE DODGE CITY AIRPORT ASOS.
THE TOP HALF OF A WOOD BARN WAS BLOWN OFF AND TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN
WINDS ESTIMATED AT NEAR 100 MPH BLEW A 20 FOOT FEATHERLITE STOCK TRAILER OVER, TIPPED OVER GRAIN CARTS AND UPROOTED A TREE 2.5 TO 3 FEET IN CIRCUMFERENCE.
SEVERAL CAMPERS WERE BLOWN OVER IN BLOOM, AND AT LEAST TWO SEMI TRAILERS WERE BLOWN OVER ALONG HIGHWAY 54 EAST OF BLOOM.
WINDOWS WERE BLOWN OUT OF A RESIDENCE BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. THE TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
HOME OWNER LOST SHINGLES OFF OF THEIR ROOF DUE TO THE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.
MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS REPORTED AT THE METHODIST CHURCH IN MULLINVILLE. SEVERAL WINDOWS WERE ALSO CRACKED OR BROKEN.
THE MILO CROP WAS STRIPPED IN SOME AREAS.
AN EMPTY 7,000 GALLON TRUCK TRAILER WAS MOVED 2 FEET. A METAL SHED WAS MOVED 5 FEET. BOTH INCIDENTS OCCURRED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF HIGHWAY 50.

THUNDERSTORMS WERE REPORTED TO HAVE DESTROYED A SHED AND BROKEN A FEW WINDOWS IN HOMES ON THE EAST SIDE OF TOWN. A 2000 GALLON EMPTY FUEL TANK WAS BLOWN INTO FARM EQUIPMENT ALSO CAUSING THE WINDOWS TO BREAK.
A 4 INCH DIAMETER TREE LIMB WAS DOWNED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF TOWN.
A 24 FOOT STOCK TRAILER AND A 500 BUSHEL GRAIN CART WERE BLOWN OVER.
DIME SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED AROUND THIS TIME AND LOCATION.
LARGE DEAD TREE WAS UPROOTED.
MOST HAIL WAS BETWEEN PING PONG TO GOLF BALL IN SIZE WITH ONLY A FEW HEN EGG TO TENNIS BALL IN SIZE.
MOSTLY SMALLER HAIL BUT A FEW PING PONG TO GOLF BALL IN SIZE.
LARGE HAY BALES WERE BLOWN OFF OF A SEMI TRAILER ON HIGHWAY 283.
2 POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.
TREE LIMBS UP TO FOUR INCHES IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN DOWN.
STRONG WINDS BLEW DOWN TREE LIMBS UP TO 14 INCHES IN DIAMETER. THE HIGH WIND WAS ACCOMPANIED BY TORRENTIAL RAIN AND APPEARS TO HAVE BEEN THE RESULT OF A WET MICROBURST.
RAIN WAS COMING STRAIGHT DOWN.
A SEMI WAS BLOWN OVER BY STRONG WIND GUSTS.
DIME SIZED HAIL ALSO OCCURRED WITH THE WIND.
HAIL SMALLER THAN PEA SIZE WAS ALSO REPORTED. THE CALLER SAW SMALL LIMBS DOWN ON THE OTHER SIDE OF HWY 50/56.
NICKEL SIZED HAIL ALSO OCCURRED WITH THE WIND.
STAFFORD COUNTY DISPATCH ADVISED A DILAPATED GARAGE AT THE SENIOR CENTER WAS KNOCKED DOWN. THE TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. RADIUM WAS ALSO REPORTED WITHOUT ELECTRIC POWER.
